meta.name: PSAS Pre-Sleep Arousal Scale
meta.description: The PSAS is a 16-item self-report measure assessing the level of arousal experienced while attempting to fall asleep. It includes two subscales: Cognitive Arousal (racing thoughts, worry, and mental alertness) and Somatic Arousal (physical sensations such as heart racing, muscle tension, and other bodily symptoms).
meta.author: Nicassio et al. (1985)
meta.reference: Nicassio PM, Mendlowitz DR, Fussell JJ, Petras L. The phenomenology of the pre-sleep state: The development of the pre-sleep arousal scale. Behav Res Ther. 1985;23(3):263-271.
meta.instruction: Please describe the degree to which each statement is experienced as you attempt to fall asleep in your bedroom.

questions.text: Review or ponder events of the day
Worry about problems other than sleep
Thoughts keep running through your head
Being distracted by sounds, noises in the environment
Heart racing, pounding, or beating irregularly
A jittery, nervous feeling in your body
Shortness of breath or labored breathing
A tight, tense feeling in your muscles
Cold feeling in your hands, feet, or body
An upset stomach (stomach knot or nervous feeling)
Perspiration in palms or other parts of body
Dry feeling in mouth or throat

ranges.label: Low Pre-Sleep Arousal
ranges.description: Your overall pre-sleep arousal is low. You generally feel calm and relaxed when trying to fall asleep.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your healthy sleep habits

ranges.label: Moderate Pre-Sleep Arousal
ranges.description: Your pre-sleep arousal is moderate. You experience some degree of mental or physical activation when trying to fall asleep.
suggestions: Limit caffeine in the afternoon and evening

ranges.label: High Pre-Sleep Arousal
ranges.description: Your pre-sleep arousal is high. You frequently experience significant mental and/or physical activation when trying to fall asleep.
suggestions: Consider cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia (CBT-I)
Practice structured worry time earlier in the evening

ranges.label: Very High Pre-Sleep Arousal
ranges.description: Your pre-sleep arousal is very high. You experience intense mental and physical activation at bedtime.
suggestions: Seek professional help from a sleep specialist or psychologist
CBT-I is the gold standard treatment for insomnia-related arousal
Discuss medication options with a healthcare provider
Address underlying anxiety or stress through professional therapy

cognitive.label: Low Cognitive Arousal
cognitive.description: Minimal mental activation before sleep. Your mind is generally calm.

cognitive.label: Moderate Cognitive Arousal
cognitive.description: Some racing thoughts or worry before sleep.

cognitive.label: High Cognitive Arousal
cognitive.description: Frequent racing thoughts, worry, and mental alertness before sleep.

cognitive.label: Very High Cognitive Arousal
cognitive.description: Intense mental activation with persistent racing thoughts that significantly disrupts sleep onset.

somatic.label: Moderate Somatic Arousal
somatic.description: Some physical tension or bodily symptoms before sleep.

somatic.label: High Somatic Arousal
somatic.description: Frequent physical symptoms such as muscle tension or heart racing before sleep.

somatic.label: Very High Somatic Arousal
somatic.description: Intense physical arousal symptoms that significantly interfere with falling asleep.
